---
title: "Sensitivity (robustness sprints 01–09)"
---

Nine post-hoc robustness analyses were performed in response to internal
critical review. None changed the pre-specified primary conclusions; all
results are listed below with effect estimates and confidence intervals.

## Sprint 1 — Negative-control outcomes

| Outcome | n DBS | n Never-DBS | Δ DBS − Never-DBS | TOST P_max | NI? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| NP1PAIN (positive ctrl) | 67 | 575 | −0.016 | 4.9 × 10⁻¹³ | ✓ |
| NP1HALL (neg ctrl) | 67 | 575 | −0.032 | 2.6 × 10⁻³¹ | ✓ |
| NP1URN (neg ctrl) | 67 | 575 | +0.125 | 1.1 × 10⁻¹¹ | ✓ |
| NP1COG (neg ctrl) | 67 | 575 | −0.058 | 1.4 × 10⁻¹⁷ | ✓ |

## Sprint 2 — Anchor sensitivity

| Anchor scheme | Δ (DBS − Never-DBS) | 95 % CI | TOST P_max | NI? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Patient anchor | −0.016 | (−0.249, +0.217) | 4.9 × 10⁻¹³ | ✓ |
| Cohort-median | −0.094 | (−0.379, +0.191) | 1.8 × 10⁻⁹ | ✓ |
| Symmetric midpoint | +0.082 | (−0.150, +0.314) | 1.0 × 10⁻¹⁴ | ✓ |

## Sprint 3 — E-values and MNAR tipping-point

| Contrast | Point estimate | 95 % CI | E-value | E-value (lower CI) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| LMM Δ slope | 0.899 | 0.843–0.958 | 1.47 | 1.26 |
| Worsener RR | 0.354 | 0.139–0.905 | 5.09 | 1.45 |

MNAR tipping-point: TOST conclusion holds up to ±0.75-point shift in
Δ Pain among dropouts; flips only at ±1.0 points.

## Sprint 4 — Network Comparison Test

| Window | DBS strength | Never-DBS strength | Global P | Max-edge P |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Baseline | 6.46 | 5.35 | 0.14 | 0.89 |
| Early-post | 4.92 | 5.41 | 0.65 | 0.11 |
| Late-post | 4.91 | 6.66 | 0.53 | **0.050** |

## Sprint 5 — Bootstrap Δρ + Brant + Firth

| Cohort | ρ DBS | ρ Never-DBS | Δρ | 95 % CI | P |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Matched (n = 89) | +0.033 | +0.192 | −0.159 | (−0.60, +0.29) | 0.48 |
| Full (n = 537) | −0.025 | +0.137 | −0.162 | (−0.47, +0.15) | 0.32 |

Brant: matched P = 0.70, full P = 0.96 → proportional-odds holds.
Wald / Profile / Firth CIs are indistinguishable.

## Sprint 6 — Cluster-robust SE + GEE AR(1)

LMM contrasts robust under CR2 SEs. GEE Pre-DBS × time interaction
sensitive to working correlation: exchangeable P = 0.079, AR(1) P = 0.59.
Post-DBS × time interaction robust under both (P > 0.5).

## Sprint 7 — PSM diagnostics

- Propensity-model c-statistic = 0.885 (excellent discrimination).
- Caliper sensitivity: n_matched(DBS/ctrl) = 87/156 (0.05), 89/166 (0.10), 95/176 (0.20). Max |SMD| 0.066, 0.105, 0.112.

## Sprint 8 — Fine-Gray competing risk

Subdistribution HR (DBS vs Never-DBS, reaching pain ≥ 2) = **1.86 (1.28–2.69)**, P = 0.001.
Cause-specific Cox HR = 1.84 (1.23–2.77), P = 0.003.
Reflects channeling pattern at baseline (DBS recipients had higher
baseline pain).

## Sprint 9 — ΔLEDD mediation

| Cohort | ACME | 95 % CI | P |
|---|---|---|---|
| Matched (n = 89) | −0.021 | (−0.091, +0.074) | 0.69 |
| Full (n = 537) | −0.040 | (−0.091, +0.003) | 0.07 |

ΔLEDD does not mediate the pain effect → stimulation-specific (not pharmacological) mechanism.
